name: Vallibona and El Turmell Loop
name_original: Tour por Els Ports — Circular a Vallibona por El Turmell
description: This difficult 28.7-mile mountain biking route through Els Ports offers panoramic views from El Turmell and passes through Vallibona.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Morella offers diverse landscapes in the mountainous province of Castellón, at an altitude of 1,000 meters. The region features a varied terrain of forests, ravines, and dramatic geological formations, particularly within the Els Ports natural landscape. This environment provides a scenic and engaging backdrop for mountain biking, with routes ranging from gentle paths to challenging trails with steep climbs and descents. Best mountain bike trails around Morella The most popular mountain bike trail is Cervoles River Springs and Waterfalls Loop, a difficult 25.6 miles (41.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural features of the Riu Cervoles, including its springs and waterfalls. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vallibona and El Turmell Loop, a difficult 28.7 miles (46.2 km) path. This route traverses the mountainous terrain, offering extensive views and challenging ascents. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Morella Castle – Santa Lucía Aqueduct loop from Hortells, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with historical landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Morella is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and unique geological formations within the Els Ports region. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult, technical trails. The routes in Morella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Morella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Morella?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Morella, offering a wide range of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Morella?
A: Morella's mountain bike trails feature diverse terrain, including mountainous landscapes, dense forests, ravines, and dramatic geological formations, particularly within the Els Ports natural region. You'll encounter both challenging climbs and fast descents.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Morella?
A: While many trails are moderate to difficult, the region offers options for various abilities. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Morella Castle – Santa Lucía Aqueduct loop from Hortells&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 23.1 miles (37.2 km) with varied landscapes and historical landmarks.

Q: What natural features can I see along the mountain bike routes?
A: Many routes showcase the region's natural beauty. You can explore the Riu Cervoles with its seasonal springs and waterfalls on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932112134/cervoles-river-springs-and-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervoles River Springs and Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1608748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Donzella Spring&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument, is also accessible on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Donzella Spring – Santa Lucía Aqueduct loop from Morella&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Yes, the area is rich in cultural and historical elements. You can encounter hermitages, farmhouses, bridges, dry stone walls, and ancient buildings. The Roman-era Aqueduct of Santa Llúcia is a visible landmark on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía Aqueduct – Morella loop from Morella&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2956080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morella Castle&lt;/a&gt; also offers breathtaking panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Morella?
A: The most pleasant times for mountain biking in Morella are during spring and autumn, when temperatures are mild and ideal for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Absolutely. Morella offers challenging trails with steep climbs and fast descents. For experienced riders, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931110078/vallibona-and-el-turmell-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallibona and El Turmell Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 28.7-mile (46.2 km) path traversing mountainous terrain with extensive views and significant ascents.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Morella?
A: The mountain bike routes in Morella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, scenic backdrops, and the network's options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that start directly from Morella town?
A: Many routes are designed to start from Morella itself, particularly from the Fàbrica de Giner. These routes are often themed to highlight local cultural or natural heritage, providing convenient access from the town.

Q: Are there any loop trails available for mountain biking?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Morella are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932112134/cervoles-river-springs-and-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervoles River Springs and Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931110078/vallibona-and-el-turmell-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallibona and El Turmell Loop&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Morella?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the trail's difficulty. Moderate routes might have around 500-700 meters of ascent, while difficult trails, such as the Vallibona and El Turmell Loop, can feature over 1,300 meters of elevation gain.
tips: Spectacular peak with impressive views where the forest observatory is located on the top of the mountain that gives it its name. From Xert, we have approximately 16.5 km of hard work, especially in the last 6 km, to reach the top, but the reward is worth it. Extraordinary panoramic views in a totally mountainous environment, isolated and wild. If we choose a day with good visibility we will fully enjoy the landscape. The climb is totally recommended.
Vallibona is a charming destination to visit! This small town, located in the Els Ports region of Castellón, has a medieval charm that will transport you back in time.
Well preserved farmhouse with the river next to it. Good place to rest, get filtered water, take a bath or even spend the night.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the 'Tour por Els Ports — Circular a Vallibona por El Turmell' mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging route features varied terrain, including singletrack, bridle paths, tracks, and firebreaks. You'll traverse diverse landscapes such as pine and holm oak forests, ledges, and scree fields. Expect descents to the Cervol riverbed and ascents through botanical trails, offering a demanding but rewarding experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this mountain bike tour?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from the picturesque village of Vallibona. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, which serves as a convenient starting and ending point for the tour.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the summit of El Turmell, offering spectacular panoramic views. You'll also pass through the charming village of Vallibona, and encounter natural features like the Masía and Crystal Clear Pools, a sinkhole, and an old ice house (nevera del Turmell).

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from El Turmell?
faqs.answer: From the summit of El Turmell (1276 m), you can enjoy breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views. On clear days, these can extend to Penyagolosa, Mont Caro, the Ebro Delta, and other significant mountains in the region.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of livestock in bucolic meadows and diverse wildlife. This is a challenging route, so ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and elevation.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for mountain biking in the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails within the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, especially in the exposed sections, while winter might bring cold weather or even snow at higher elevations like El Turmell.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail, especially in Vallibona?
faqs.answer: Yes, the village of Vallibona, which serves as the starting point, is a charming medieval village. It offers some local amenities, including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options, making it a great base for your adventure.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Camino del Maestrazgo-Bajo Aragón, El Camí IT6TR3 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, and Camí de Conquesta, among others like Camí del Turmell and Ruta Botànica.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging mountain bike tour?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult classification and length (~46 km with ~1393 m elevation gain), you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate clothing layers, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: What makes this mountain bike trail particularly notable?
faqs.answer: This trail stands out for its genuinely difficult mountain biking challenge, suitable for experienced riders. It offers exceptional natural beauty within the Els Ports region and Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park, providing diverse ecosystems and breathtaking panoramic views from El Turmell. The route also passes through the culturally rich village of Vallibona, offering a sense of discovery in a secluded and authentic setting.
